name: Oriental Stuffed Chicken Rolls with Sweet-Sour
recipeIngredient: 8 ounces Water chestnuts, drained and chopped
3/4 cup Bean sprouts, rinsed and drained
2 tablespoons Cornstarch mixed with 1/4 cup water
2 Eggs, slightly beaten with 2 tablespoons water
recipeInstructions:
1. Pound chicken breasts between pieces of waxed paper using a mallet or the broad side of a cleaver until thin.
2. Dredge the pounded chicken in buttermilk baking mix and set aside.
3. In a large skillet, sauté onions in oil until tender.
4. Add shrimp, water chestnuts, bean sprouts, soy sauce, and chicken bouillon to the skillet. Cook and stir until the shrimp are opaque.
5. Stir in the cornstarch mixture; cook and stir until thickened.
6. Place 2 tablespoons of filling at one end of each chicken breast half.
7. Roll up the chicken breasts securely with toothpicks.
8. Dip each roll in the egg mixture, then roll in the remaining buttermilk baking mix.
9. Heat oil for deep-frying to 400°F (200°C).
10. Fry the rolls in batches of two for about 10 minutes or until golden brown. Drain on paper towels and keep warm in a 300°F (150°C) oven until serving time.
11. Serve with Sweet-sour Apricot Sauce.
NOTE: Chicken can be pounded and dredged, and the filling can be made up to one day ahead. Refrigerate until ready to use. The sauce keeps refrigerated for at least one month. To prepare the sauce, combine all ingredients in a small saucepan. Stir over medium-low heat until sugar dissolves. Serve warm or at room temperature.
